product_name: Clinical AI Safety Ops / Health AI Assurance Kit

schema_fields.name: digest_id
schema_fields.meaning: Stable row id for a manually reviewed monitoring signal.

schema_fields.name: date_checked
schema_fields.meaning: Date when the source was manually checked.

schema_fields.name: signal_surface
schema_fields.meaning: Benchmark, model release, governance, source support, language drift, adapter route, repo health, or outreach route.

schema_fields.name: source_locator
schema_fields.meaning: Local file path or explicit source pointer used for the row.

schema_fields.name: observed_change
schema_fields.meaning: What changed or what needs attention, written as a bounded internal note.

schema_fields.name: evidence_status
schema_fields.meaning: local_artifact, official_source_needed, user_approval_needed, blocked, or no_change.

schema_fields.name: action_meaning
schema_fields.meaning: What this means for the Health AI Assurance Kit roadmap.

schema_fields.name: risk_tags
schema_fields.meaning: Controlled tags such as source_support, language_drift, external_claim, adapter_route, or automation_boundary.

schema_fields.name: blocked_claims
schema_fields.meaning: Claims that must not be made from this row.

schema_fields.name: external_action_gate
schema_fields.meaning: blocked_without_user_approval for any external post, email, PR, comment, provider run, or institution named claim.

watch_surfaces.manual_question: Did a benchmark or eval ecosystem signal change the local boundary language?
Did a medical or policy claim need exact source support review?
Did Turkish or English wording drift create a safety review need?
watch_surfaces.allowed_action: update internal drift note without adding new cases

watch_surfaces.manual_question: Did an adapter route need a local wrapper, smoke test, or blocked external packet?
Did the demo or panel review state need an internal update?
watch_surfaces.allowed_action: update local demo index or review status only

watch_surfaces.manual_question: Did a governance or network route need a bounded next action?
Did validators, CI, or public claim hygiene change?
watch_surfaces.allowed_action: run local QA and update BAGLAM2

sample_digest_rows.observed_change: Boundary index lists benchmark lanes and blocked claims for HealthBench, MedHELM, MedFailBench, SourceCheckup, and Turkish Clinical SafetyBench.
sample_digest_rows.action_meaning: Use benchmark signals as design lenses, not as compatibility or endorsement claims.

sample_digest_rows.observed_change: Monitoring plan exists but automation is not started.
sample_digest_rows.action_meaning: Keep monitoring as manual digest rows until the owner explicitly asks to start automation.

sample_digest_rows.observed_change: Kit card connects eight local evidence layers and keeps the external gate blocked.
sample_digest_rows.action_meaning: Use the kit card as the internal anchor for digest interpretation.

sample_digest_rows.observed_change: Clinician literacy demo index has a 20 minute, three case, six step local flow.
sample_digest_rows.action_meaning: Route education signals into local demo updates, not external presentation claims.

sample_digest_rows.observed_change: SourceCheckup CLI keeps source presence separate from exact claim support.
sample_digest_rows.action_meaning: Any source bearing medical claim should become an internal review row before public wording.

sample_digest_rows.observed_change: Turkish drift dashboard keeps two validation tiers, five TR EN pairs, and 44 existing Turkish synthetic rows.
sample_digest_rows.action_meaning: Language drift signals stay separated by validation tier and do not create new case claims.

next_safe_actions: connect this schema to the roadmap as P7 completed
keep P8 external proof route blocked without explicit user approval
use digest rows only as internal BAGLAM2 style decision support
